title: ACB v Thomson Medical Pte Ltd
text: Proof-of-concept sandbox summary (paraphrased from public information; not authoritative text). The case arose from an in-vitro fertilisation mix-up. The Singapore Court of Appeal recognised a head of damage described as loss of genetic affinity, while declining to allow a claim for the ordinary costs of raising a healthy child as upkeep damages. ACB v Thomson Medical is a notable Singapore authority in the law of negligence and damages.
